title: CLI Scaffold and Config Management

story.i_want_to: install and configure the loopctl CLI escript binary with server URL, API key, and output format preferences
story.so_that: all subsequent CLI commands can authenticate with the loopctl server without repeating credentials on every invocation

rationale: The CLI is the primary interface for both human developers and AI agents to interact with loopctl. A well-structured scaffold with config management, environment variable overrides, and multiple output formats (json default for agents, human and csv for humans) is the foundation that every other CLI command depends on. This story establishes the escript build, the config file layout, the HTTP client setup, and the command routing infrastructure.

acceptance_criteria.description: The project compiles to an escript binary named 'loopctl' via mix escript.build
Config is stored at ~/.loopctl/config.json with keys: server, api_key, format. The directory and file are created automatically on first config set
Environment variables LOOPCTL_SERVER, LOOPCTL_API_KEY, and LOOPCTL_FORMAT override config file values when set
'loopctl config set <key> <value>' writes a key-value pair to the config file
'loopctl config get <key>' reads and prints a single config value
'loopctl config show' prints the full resolved configuration (file + env overrides)
Default output format is 'json' (agent-first). Supports --format json, --format human, and --format csv as global flags on any command
Uses Req as the HTTP client for all API communication, with configurable base URL from the server config value
'loopctl' with no arguments or 'loopctl --help' prints usage information listing all available top-level commands
All commands that require server communication fail with a clear error message if server and api_key are not configured (either via config file or environment variables)

test_cases.name: Config set creates config file and directory
preconditions: No existing config file at the test config path
Test uses a temporary directory instead of ~/.loopctl
steps: Read the config file from the temporary path
expected_results: Config file is created at tmp_path/config.json

test_cases.name: Environment variables override config file values
expected_results: Environment values take precedence over file values

test_cases.name: Config show displays resolved configuration
expected_results: Output indicates which values come from env overrides (if any)

test_cases.name: Global --format flag is parsed and propagated
expected_results: Parsed result has options containing project: 'my-proj'

test_cases.name: Missing config produces clear error for server commands
preconditions: No LOOPCTL_SERVER or LOOPCTL_API_KEY environment variables set
expected_results: Returns {:error, message} where message indicates server and api_key must be configured

test_cases.name: Help text is displayed for no arguments
expected_results: Output lists available commands: config, auth, tenant, keys, project, import, export, status, next, blocked, agent, claim, start, report, unclaim, verify, reject, pending, state, history, audit, changes, webhook, admin

test_cases.name: HTTP client is configured with base URL and auth header
preconditions: Config resolved with server='https://loopctl.example.com' and api_key='lc_test123'
expected_results: Returns a Req.Request struct with base_url set to 'https://loopctl.example.com/api/v1'
Request has Authorization header set to 'Bearer lc_test123'
Request has Content-Type header set to 'application/json'

technical_notes: Configure escript in mix.exs with main_module: Loopctl.CLI.Main and escript name: :loopctl
Use a command router pattern: Loopctl.CLI.Main.main/1 dispatches to command-specific modules based on the first argument(s)
Config module should accept an optional config_path parameter to enable testing without touching ~/.loopctl
Use Jason for JSON parsing/encoding of the config file
Req client should be built once per command invocation and passed through to API-calling functions
Output formatting should be handled by a Loopctl.CLI.Formatter module with format/2 that accepts data and format type (:json, :human, :csv)
The escript needs to include all dependencies (Req, Jason, etc.) — verify with mix escript.build that all deps are bundled
Global flags (--format, --help) should be extracted before dispatching to command modules
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
CLI HTTP client MUST be injectable via behaviour or Req.Test for test isolation. Use Req.Test.expect/3 for HTTP mocking in tests. Never use Application.put_env in test files.
